prompt: Matrix multiplication gives wrong results. The output matrix has correct dimensions but values are wrong. Simple 2x2 * 2x2 test shows the issue clearly.

<task>
Matrix multiplication gives wrong results. The output matrix has correct dimensions but values are wrong. Simple 2x2 * 2x2 test shows the issue clearly.

arguments.reasoning: Found the bug at line 34: `other.data[j][k]` should be `other.data[k][j]`. In matrix multiplication C = A * B, element C[i][j] = sum(A[i][k] * B[k][j]) for k. The current code accesses B[j][k] instead of B[k][j], which transposes the access pattern for the second matrix.

arguments.summary: Found the bug in src/matrix.rs:34. In the inner loop `sum += self.data[i][k] * other.data[j][k]` — the indices for `other` are swapped. Should be `other.data[k][j]` not `other.data[j][k]`. Matrix multiplication C[i][j] = sum(A[i][k] * B[k][j]).

arguments.final_answer: Fixed matrix multiplication bug in src/matrix.rs:34. The inner loop accessed `other.data[j][k]` instead of `other.data[k][j]` — the row and column indices for the second matrix were swapped. For C[i][j] = sum(A[i][k] * B[k][j]), the code was computing C[i][j] = sum(A[i][k] * B[j][k]) which is effectively multiplying by B-transpose. All 3 tests pass.
